title: IX3244-B
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}}
{{Youmay|the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] [[Survey Team]]|[[Korkar]], a [[moon]] of [[Endor (planet)|Endor]]}}

{{Quote|My team, under the command of Captain Toss, visited several other worlds before our survey frigate arrived at Endor's moon, far out on the fringes.|Kiviett describing IX3244-B's travels before finding Endor|<ref name="ISP" />}}
'''IX3244-B''' was an [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] [[Survey Team]] under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Toss]], with [[Planetologist/Legends|planetologist]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Kiviett]] as second-in-command. The team was one of the Survey Teams that attempted to find a construction site for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], and IX3244-B found the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] as a potential location. As their survey [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] Endor, Toss sent Kiviett and others to investigate the life forms detected on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. While the IX3244-B members on Endor were spooked by the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], Toss ultimately dismissed the concerns and went on to publish reports about Endor being suitable as a [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] construction site.

However, the superficial nature of IX3244-B's reports resulted in [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] sending another Survey Team led by [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Pfilbee Jhorn]] to Endor. Jhorn additionally viewed IX3244-B as disgraceful for failing to note such dangers as [[Gorax/Legends|Gorax]], [[Condor dragon/Legends|condor dragons]], and [[Sanyassan Marauders]] on Endor. Following [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY's]] [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Kiviett was still serving as IX3244-B's second-in-command. He was captured by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and offered to join them as a planetologist.

==Description==
IX3244-B was an [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] [[Survey Team]] active by some [[Time/Legends|time]] following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. The team's transportation included a survey [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] and at least one [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]].<ref name="ISP" />

==History==
===Surveying Endor===
{{Quote|All data indicated a diverse geosphere, though dominated by forests, that could very comfortably sustain Human life. This was precisely what Lord Vader was looking for.|Kiviett, describing IX3244-B's scans of Endor|<ref name="ISP" />}}
[[File:Moon_of_Endor_NEGAS.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The Forest Moon of Endor, where IX3244-B would discover and survey for Mission 759/B]]
When [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] planned the construction of the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], IX3244-B was one of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] Survey Teams that went to search for locations that would be viable for the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] construction. The team visited several [[Planet/Legends|planets]] before discovering the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]<ref name="ISP" /> in an event that would be reported as [[Mission 759/B]].<ref name="SWS">{{WEGCite|story=Report to Imperial Command|book=The Star Wars Sourcebook}}</ref>

Endor was a remote location, which satisfied what [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] was seeking as a location for a Death Star [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] construction site. IX3244-B's scanners also detected the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] could sustain [[Human/Legends|Human]] life, which Vader also wanted.<ref name="ISP" />

===Mission 759/B and later Imperial defeat===
{{Quote|We didn't make it to within a kilometer of the tree house city.|Kiviett mentions how he and others in IX3244-B failed to reach Bright Tree Village|<ref name="ISP" />}}
[[File:VictoryOnEndor.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Ewoks, who led IX3244-B in circles when the Survey Team attempted to reach their Bright Tree Village]]
While IX3244-B's frigate remained in Endor's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], further scans showed many primitive life forms on the moon. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Toss]], the [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], sent his second-in-command, [[Planetologist/Legends|planetologist]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Kiviett]], and others from the team to the moon to investigate. After landing on Endor, Kiviett and the other team members attempted to reach<ref name="ISP" /> the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks']] [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]].<ref name="FF">{{FactFile|11|END 7-10|The Battle of Endor}}</ref> The Ewoks, however, led them in circles and confused even the team's best [[Scout/Legends|scouts]]. Ultimately unable to reach the Ewok [[City/Legends|village]], the IX3244-B members decided to retreat back to the frigate after more than two [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] of wandering in circles.<ref name="ISP" />

When Kiviett reported the team's trouble from the Ewoks to Toss, the latter dismissed the concerns, viewing Endor as a great site for the Death Star's construction.<ref name="ISP" /> However, Palpatine was dissatisfied with the superficial nature of IX3244-B's reports and later sent another Survey Team to Endor. That team was led by [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Pfilbee Jhorn]], who viewed IX3244-B as disgraces to the Imperial Military for failing to mention the presence of dangers such as [[Gorax/Legends|Gorax]], [[Condor dragon/Legends|condor dragons]], and [[Sanyassan Marauders]].<ref name="The Illustrated Star Wars Universe">''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''</ref> Ultimately, [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY's]]<ref name="FF" /> [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] led to the Ewoks helping the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] defeat the Empire. Kiviett was still serving as IX3244-B's second-in-command when he was captured by the Alliance, and he recounted his experience with the Survey Team while talking to Alliance [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] and offering to join the Alliance as a planetologist.<ref name="ISP" /> At another point after the Battle of Endor, Na'al had also interviewed the Ewok [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]], publishing the interview where the Ewok talked about his own personal experiences with IX3244-B's attempt to reach Bright Tree Village, along with later encounters with the Empire and defeating the Empire in the Battle of Endor.<ref name="GG5">{{WEGCite|story=The Ewok Defenses|book=Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi}}</ref>

==Commanders and crew==
IX3244-B was led by the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] Captain Toss, and his second-in-command was Lieutenant Kiviett, a planetologist. Including Kiviett, the Survey Team had at least two [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and three men with light [[Armor/Legends|armor]].<ref name="ISP" />

==Behind the scenes==
IX3244-B was created for "[[Report to Imperial Command]]," a short narrative included in [[1987]]'s ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'', authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] and [[Curtis Smith]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="SWS" /> The unit was expanded upon in "[[Imperial Scouting Party]]," a story written by [[Michael Stern]] and released in [[1990]]'s ''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]'', also from West End Games.<ref name="ISP" />

The Survey Team would again be mentioned in [[1995]]'s ''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''<ref name="The Illustrated Star Wars Universe" /> before being mentioned in [[2002]]'s [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 11|eleventh issue]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]''. That source mentioned Pfilbee Jhorn being a part of IX3244-B, placing him in events from "Imperial Scouting Party,"<ref name="FF" /> although ''The Illustrated Star Wars Universe'' had previously established Jhorn led the team that went to Endor after IX3244-B. This article assumes that ''Fact File'' 11 was in error<ref name="The Illustrated Star Wars Universe" /> and that Jhorn was not with the initial group during the events previously elaborated upon by "Imperial Scouting Party."<ref name="ISP" />
